article_title: Starcloud Raises $170 Million to Build Data Centers in Space, Reaching Unicorn Status 17 Months After Y Combinator

content_preview.title: Starcloud Raises $170 Million to Build Data Centers in Space, Reaching Unicorn Status 17 Months After Y Combinator
content_preview.summary: The Redmond, Washington-based startup closed a $170 million Series A led by Benchmark and EQT Ventures, valuing the company at $1.1 billion as it prepares to launch more powerful orbital compute satellites carrying Nvidia Blackwell chips.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Starcloud, a startup building data centers aboard satellites in low Earth orbit, has raised $170 million in a Series A round that values the company at $1.1 billion. The round was led by Benchmark and EQT Ventures, with participation from Macquarie Capital, NFX, Nebular, Y Combinator, Adjacent, 776 Ventures, Fuse Ventures, Manhattan West, and Monolith Power Systems, [according to TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/30/starcloud-raises-170-million-series-ato-build-data-centers...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong startup funding article covering orbital data center concept with technical detail and competitive context.
editor_notes.source_verification: 3 sources verified via source snapshots (TechCrunch, The Next Web, SpaceNews). All captured by automated review.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Claims verified: $170M Series A, $1.1B valuation, Benchmark/EQT lead, YC graduate, Starcloud-1 Nov 2025, H100 GPU, Blackwell for Starcloud-2.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral. Covers both ambitions and skepticism about orbital compute viability.
editor_notes.originality: No prior orbital data center coverage. New topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid startup article. Sources verified. Approved.
